subject: Gaming
title: PS1: Gaming Grows Up
description: A short lesson to help you apply PS1: Gaming Grows Up.

hook.text: Sony didn't just make a console. They made gaming cool for adults. Clubs played PS1 demos at parties.

buildup.text: Sony marketed PlayStation in nightclubs, music magazines, and fashion outlets—not toy stores.

discovery.text: Games like Resident Evil, Metal Gear Solid, and Tomb Raider attracted audiences who never owned a Nintendo.

twist.text: CDs were cheap to produce. Developers flocked to PlayStation because cartridges cost ten times more.

climax.text: PlayStation redefined who played games. Gaming shifted from children's hobby to adult entertainment.

punchline.text: Nightclubs and fashion. Sony made gaming cool for everyone.

quiz.question: How did Sony market the PlayStation differently?
options: Through nightclubs, music magazines, and fashion outlets
